methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $10.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $1.8B.

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: Collaborative Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M+

private.name: Fairmat
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $350M

private.name: GrayMatter Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $300M+

data.narrative:
1X Technologies, a Series B robotics and manufacturing company, presents a valuation assessment against a peer set of five same-stage private companies in the sector (Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, Collaborative Robotics, Fairmat, GrayMatter Robotics) plus one public comparable.

Two methods triangulate the valuation. The Stage-Progression approach applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $10.0B last post-money valuation, implying $19.0B. The Peer-Median method benchmarks against the five robotics comparables with a median valuation of $500M and a range spanning $300M to $1.8B. The peer sample shows material dispersion: Bedrock and Dexterity trade at $1.8B and $1.6B respectively, while Fairmat and GrayMatter cluster near $300–$350M.

The analysis produces a triangulated range of $425M (low) to $21.9B (high) with a median of $9.8B. The wide spread reflects tension between the peer-median method, which implies substantial undervaluation at current terms, and stage-progression logic anchored to the reported $10B valuation. Key uncertainties include whether 1X's capital efficiency, technology differentiation, or market traction warrant a premium to peer medians, and whether the reported $10B valuation reflects genuine market consensus or contains execution optionality priced in. The $125M raised to date against the stated $10B valuation suggests early-stage capital intensity assumptions warrant scrutiny.
